steps.title: Identify the contrast placement
steps.content: The sentence contrasts the bright supernova dimming after a few weeks with the fact that supernovas, despite being powerful, are short-lived. The word "however" belongs with the first clause as a parenthetical contrast marker.

steps.title: Join the clauses cleanly
steps.content: Choice A and choice C place "however" before an "although" clause, creating a clumsy double contrast. Choice D uses only commas between independent clauses. The remaining choice sets off "however" after "weeks" and uses a semicolon before the next independent clause. The answer is B.
